strategy: The Gold Dragon takes 75% of the hits aimed at the Sultan (Shadow Sentinel) and has -30% incoming, so single-target hits mostly land on the Dragon - deal with it first or hit AoE.
Do not rely on single Feints: he Cleanse Wards himself twice to wipe negative wards, which even beats Indemnity.
Use AoE traps like Windstorm instead - they do not trigger his double cleanse, so your traps stick.
Expect a +35% Darkwind (accuracy debuff) after several rounds, followed by an Iron Sultan hit on the lowest-health wizard - keep everyone topped and consider accuracy support.
Set up with AoE traps and blades, then clear the Gold Dragon and Steeleton along with the Sultan in one AoE hit through his 8,800 health.
cheatNotes: Join late and he casts the Shadow version of Iron Sultan - arrive on time.
On Round 1 the Gold Dragon gives itself a Shadow Sentinel: 75% intercept, 100 critical block, and -30% incoming, so it soaks single hits meant for the Sultan.
He casts Cleanse Ward twice on himself to strip all negative wards, so Indemnity will not save a Feint.
AoE traps such as Windstorm do NOT trigger that double cleanse - use them to keep traps on him.
After several rounds he casts a +35% Darkwind, cutting the team's accuracy.
After the global he casts Iron Sultan on whoever has the lowest health - keep everyone topped up.
